summary:
• Jamaica's offshore oil development and U.S. drilling pace increases represent supply growth risks in a market facing potential structural tightness, as global demand pressures could outpace new production additions.

• Geopolitical tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, Iran nuclear negotiations, and Russia-China-Iran alignment create acute supply disruption risks for critical Persian Gulf flows that support 20+ million barrels per day of global trade.

• Regulatory pressures including UK sanctions on Russian crude processing, fuel tax changes across six U.S. states, and European concerns over LNG dependence are fragmenting markets and potentially constraining refining and distribution capacity when spare capacity is already thin.
